# Interface: ChartPropertiesOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartPropertiesOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.ChartPropertiesOverrides

Property overrides that can be used with [IChartingLibraryWidget.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#applyoverrides)
.

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartPropertiesOverrides/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`StudyOverrides`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyOverrides)
    
    ↳ **`ChartPropertiesOverrides`**

### mainSeriesProperties.minTick[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartPropertiesOverrides/#mainseriespropertiesmintick "Direct link to mainSeriesProperties.minTick")

• **mainSeriesProperties.minTick**: `string`

Main series minimum tick behaviour.

**`Default`**

    "default"

**`Example`**

    // reset minTick to defaulttvWidget.applyOverrides({ 'mainSeriesProperties.minTick': 'default' });// Set main series minTick to { priceScale: 10000, minMove: 1, frac: false }tvWidget.applyOverrides({ 'mainSeriesProperties.minTick': '10000,1,false' });// Set default minTick for overlay studies to { priceScale: 10000, minMove: 1, frac: false }tvWidget.applyStudiesOverrides({ 'overlay.minTick': '10000,1,false' });

* * *

• **mainSeriesProperties.sessionId**: `"regular"` | `"extended"`

Sessions to display on the chart. Use `'extended'` to include pre- and post-market subsessions. See the [Extended Sessions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/connecting_data/Extended-Sessions)
 guide for more info.

**`Default`**

    'regular'

# Interface: CommentLineToolOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CommentLineToolOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.CommentLineToolOverrides

Override properties for the Comment drawing tool.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CommentLineToolOverrides/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### linetoolcomment.backgroundColor[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CommentLineToolOverrides/#linetoolcommentbackgroundcolor "Direct link to linetoolcomment.backgroundColor")

• **linetoolcomment.backgroundColor**: `string`

Default value: `#2962FF`

* * *

• `Optional` **text**: `string`

Text for drawing

# Interface: ContextMenuPosition | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.ContextMenuPosition

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### attachToXBy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#attachtoxby "Direct link to attachToXBy")

• `Optional` **attachToXBy**: `"auto"` | `"left"` | `"right"`

Tells what side of the context menu widget should be used to "attach" to a provided x coordinate. If the value is `undefined`, then you may treat it based on whether it is rtl or not (e.g. `'right'` for rtl and `'left'` otherwise). The value `'auto'` behaves as `undefined` but additionally checks if there is enough space to place the menu and if it's not then the result value is inverted.

* * *

### attachToYBy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#attachtoyby "Direct link to attachToYBy")

• `Optional` **attachToYBy**: `"auto"` | `"top"` | `"bottom"` | `"auto-strict"`

Tells what side of the context menu widget should be used to "attach" to a provided y coordinate:

*   `'auto'` means similar to `'top'` but the menu could be expanded above the coordinate if needed (if there is no enough space to place it below)
*   `'auto-strict'` means `'top'` if the whole menu fits the space below the coordinate and `'bottom'` otherwise (see [box](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition#box)
    )
*   `'top'` means that the menu should be placed to the bottom of y coordinate (the menu should be attached by its bottom to y coordinate)
*   `'bottom'` means that the menu should be placed above y coordinate (the menu should be attached by its top to y coordinate)

You may treat `undefined` as `'auto'`.

* * *

### box[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#box "Direct link to box")

• `Optional` **box**: `Object`

The optional structure that helps to more accurate calculate a position of the menu (see [attachToYBy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition#attachtoyby)
).

#### Type declaration[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#type-declaration "Direct link to Type declaration")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `h` | `number` | menu height |
| `overlapX?` | `boolean` | x coordinate overlaps |
| `w` | `number` | menu width |
| `x` | `number` | menu x coordinate |
| `y` | `number` | menu y coordinate |

* * *

### clientX[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#clientx "Direct link to clientX")

• **clientX**: `number`

X (horizontal) coordinate (in pixels) at which the mouse event occurred, relative to the left edge of the applications viewport.

* * *

### clientY[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#clienty "Direct link to clientY")

• **clientY**: `number`

Y (vertical) coordinate (in pixels) at which the mouse event occurred, relative to the left edge of the applications viewport.

* * *

### marginX[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#marginx "Direct link to marginX")

• `Optional` **marginX**: `number`

Additional horizontal margin.

* * *

### marginY[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#marginy "Direct link to marginY")

• `Optional` **marginY**: `number`

Additional vertical margin.

* * *

### touches[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#touches "Direct link to touches")

• `Optional` **touches**: readonly { `clientX`: `number` ; `clientY`: `number` }\[\]

Touch positions

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#properties)
    *   [attachToXBy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#attachtoxby)
        
    *   [attachToYBy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#attachtoyby)
        
    *   [box](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#box)
        
    *   [clientX](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#clientx)
        
    *   [clientY](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#clienty)
        
    *   [marginX](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#marginx)
        
    *   [marginY](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#marginy)
        
    *   [touches](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuPosition/#touches)

# Interface: ContextMenuOptions | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuOptions/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.ContextMenuOptions

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuOptions/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### items\_processor[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuOptions/#items_processor "Direct link to items_processor")

• `Optional` **items\_processor**: [`ContextMenuItemsProcessor`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#contextmenuitemsprocessor)

Provide this function if you want to change the set of actions being displayed in the context menu.

You can filter out, add yours and re-order items.

The library will call your function each time it wants to display a context menu and will provide a list of items to display. This function should return an array of items to display.

Example:

    context_menu: {  items_processor: function(items, actionsFactory, params) {     console.log(`Menu name is: ${params.menuName}`);     const newItem = actionsFactory.createAction({        actionId: 'hello-world',        label: 'Say Hello',        onExecute: function() {           alert('Hello World');        },     });     items.unshift(newItem);     return Promise.resolve(items);  },},

* * *

### renderer\_factory[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuOptions/#renderer_factory "Direct link to renderer_factory")

• `Optional` **renderer\_factory**: [`ContextMenuRendererFactory`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#contextmenurendererfactory)

**Note:** This API is experimental and might be changed significantly in the future releases. By providing this function you could override the default renderer for context menu.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuOptions/#properties)
    *   [items\_processor](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuOptions/#items_processor)
        
    *   [renderer\_factory](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuOptions/#renderer_factory)

---

# Interface: AccumulativeSwingIndexIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.AccumulativeSwingIndexIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Accumulative Swing Index' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccumulativeSwingIndexIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **asi.color**: `string`

Default value: `#2196F3`

* * *

### asi.display[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccumulativeSwingIndexIndicatorOverrides/#asidisplay "Direct link to asi.display")

• **asi.display**: `number`

Default value: `15`

* * *

### asi.linestyle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccumulativeSwingIndexIndicatorOverrides/#asilinestyle "Direct link to asi.linestyle")

• **asi.linestyle**: `number`

Default value: `0`

* * *

### asi.linewidth[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccumulativeSwingIndexIndicatorOverrides/#asilinewidth "Direct link to asi.linewidth")

• **asi.linewidth**: `number`

Default value: `1`

* * *

### asi.plottype[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccumulativeSwingIndexIndicatorOverrides/#asiplottype "Direct link to asi.plottype")

• **asi.plottype**: [`LineStudyPlotStyleName`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Charting_Library#linestudyplotstylename)

Default value: `line`

* * *

### asi.trackprice[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccumulativeSwingIndexIndicatorOverrides/#asitrackprice "Direct link to asi.trackprice")

• **asi.transparency**: `number`

• `Optional` **checkable**: `boolean`

Whether menu action represents a checkbox state. Set it to true if you need a checkbox.

* * *

• `Optional` **checked**: `boolean`

• `Optional` **externalLink**: `boolean`

* * *

• `Optional` **icon**: `string`

* * *

• `Optional` **shortcut**: `string`

Specify an icon to render - Only icons listed [here](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/customization/overrides/Shapes-and-Overrides)
 are supported

Should drawing be locked

An entity ID that can be used to associate the drawing with a study.
