# Toolbars | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/Toolbars/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v27**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

### Buttons[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/Toolbars/#buttons "Direct link to Buttons")

You can add your own buttons to the top toolbar.

[`createButton(options)`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#createbutton)

[`createDropdown(options)`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#createdropdown)

#### Accessible Buttons[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/Toolbars/#accessible-buttons "Direct link to Accessible Buttons")

Custom buttons created with TradingView's built-in style using [the `createButton` overload](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#createbutton)
 will benefit from the library's [accessible keyboard navigation added in version 26.002](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/releases/release-notes#version-26002)
.

Custom buttons created using the [non-TradingView style `createButton` overload](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#createbutton)
 will not benefit from keyboard accessibility features.

### Styles[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/Toolbars/#styles "Direct link to Styles")

The library has 2 predefined color themes. They are optimal for displaying the charts. However, since version 16 you can change the colors of the toolbars to perfectly fit to your background. You can see an example of the customization in [themes.html](https://github.com/tradingview/charting_library/blob/master/themed.html "The repository is private.")
 🔐 (access is [restricted](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/getting_started/quick-start#getting-access "Click to open the 'Getting Access' section.")
).

*   [Buttons](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/Toolbars/#buttons)
    
*   [Styles](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/Toolbars/#styles)

---

# Drawings API | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v27**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

This article describes how to manage drawings using the API.

info

Methods listed in this article are part of the [`IChartWidgetApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi)
 interface. To call these methods, you should use an `IChartWidgetApi` instance. To do this, use either the [`chart`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/core_concepts/widget-methods#chart)
 or [`activeChart`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/core_concepts/widget-methods#activechart)
 method. For more information, refer to [Widget methods](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/core_concepts/widget-methods)
.

Create drawings[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#create-drawings "Direct link to Create drawings")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you create a drawing, you need to specify its position on the chart. The position can be determined with either one or multiple points. To create a drawing, use one of the methods below depending on the drawing type and number of points it requires.

### createShape[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createshape "Direct link to createShape")

The [`createShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createshape)
 method allows you to create a drawing which position on the chart can be determined by only one point. For example, you can create arrows, vertical or horizontal lines, icons, and more. To do this, call `createShape` and pass information about the point and drawing as parameters.

#### Point[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#point "Direct link to Point")

You should specify one of the objects below to provide information about the point.

*   [`StickedPoint`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StickedPoint)
    . A point is determined by an OHLC price on a bar at a specified timestamp.
*   [`PricedPoint`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PricedPoint)
    . A point is determined by a price and timestamp.
*   [`TimePoint`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimePoint)
    . A point is determined by a timestamp.

When you specify a drawing point, use timestamps that have associated data points on the chart. Otherwise, the library adjusts the time value to the nearest appropriate point.

For example, the current chart resolution is one hour, and the following bars are displayed on the chart: `[09:00, 10:00, 11:00, ...]`. If you specify a drawing at 09:30, the library shifts it to 09:00. Moreover, if you change the resolution to 30 minutes and the data point at 09:30 appears, the library will still display the drawing at 09:00.

#### Drawing[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#drawing "Direct link to Drawing")

You should specify a [`CreateShapeOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateShapeOptions)
 object to provide information about the drawing, such as [drawing type](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateShapeOptions#shape)
 and [overrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateShapeOptions#overrides)
, and configure user control over drawings. For example, you can use the [`lock`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateShapeOptions#lock)
 property to disable moving and deleting drawings in the UI.

Consider the code sample below that creates a play icon.

    widget.chart().createShape(    { time: 1514796562, price: 150 },    {      shape: "icon",      icon: 0xf0da,    })

Note that the [`icon`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateShapeOptions#icon)
 value should be a **hex number**, not a string.

### createMultipointShape[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createmultipointshape "Direct link to createMultipointShape")

The [`createMultipointShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createmultipointshape)
 method allows you to create a drawing which position on the chart is determined by multiple points. For example, you can create triangles, trend lines, rectangles, and more. To do this, call `createMultipointShape` and pass an array of [points](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#point)
 and a [`CreateMultipointShapeOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateMultipointShapeOptions)
 object as parameters.

tip

You can check the number of required points in the _Settings_ dialog of a certain drawing. If you pass a wrong number of points, you get the following issue: _Error: Wrong points count for "drawing\_name". Required "number"_.

Consider the CodePen below that displays [_Parallel Channel_](https://www.tradingview.com/ideas/parallelchannel/?solution=43000518117)
 and _Info Line_ right after the chart is loaded.

<p>See the Pen <a href="https://codepen.io/tradingview/pen/GReRmPj"> Creating multipoint drawings</a> by TradingView (<a href="https://codepen.io/tradingview">@tradingview</a>) on <a href="https://codepen.io">CodePen</a>.</p>

### createExecutionShape[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createexecutionshape "Direct link to createExecutionShape")

The [`createExecutionShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createexecutionshape)
 method allows you to display an execution drawing on the chart. The execution is represented with buy/sell arrows. Unlike other types of drawings, users cannot change or move the execution drawings in the UI.

The `createExecutionShape` method returns an instance of the [`IExecutionLineAdapter`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IExecutionLineAdapter)
 interface that provides an extensive API for adjusting the drawing settings. For example, you can specify an arrow color and text. Note that you cannot change the arrow size.

tip

If execution drawings created via `createExecutionShape` do not meet your requirements, you can emulate these drawings using the [`createShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createshape)
 method.

Consider the CodePen below that demonstrates how to display buy/sell arrows using `createExecutionShape`and `createShape`. These arrows represent trading history.

<p>See the Pen <a href="https://codepen.io/tradingview/pen/YzgzQYV"> Drawings. Buy/Sell arrows</a> by TradingView (<a href="https://codepen.io/tradingview">@tradingview</a>) on <a href="https://codepen.io">CodePen</a>.</p>

### createAnchoredShape[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createanchoredshape "Direct link to createAnchoredShape")

The [`createAnchoredShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createanchoredshape)
 method allows you to create an anchored drawing. These drawings are fixed on the same position and do not move when a user scrolls the chart. The drawing points are measured as percentage from the top-left corner of the chart.

To create an anchored drawing, call the `createAnchoredShape` method and pass a [`PositionPercents`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionPercents)
 and [`CreateAnchoredShapeOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateAnchoredShapeOptions)
 objects as parameters. For example, the code sample below creates an anchored text.

    widget.activeChart().createAnchoredShape(    { x: 0.1, y: 0.3 },    { shape: "anchored_text", text: "This is an anchored drawing", overrides: { color: "green" }});

![Anchored drawing](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/assets/images/anchored-drawing-194baa17ff0884270d082f959c24d65a.gif)

Attach drawing to indicator[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#attach-drawing-to-indicator "Direct link to Attach drawing to indicator")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can attach a drawing to a certain indicator on the chart. To do this, specify the `ownerStudyId` property when you create a drawing using the methods [above](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#create-drawings)
. For example, the code sample below attaches an icon to the [_Exponential Moving Average (EMA)_](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000592270-exponential-moving-average/)
 indicator.

    // Create the EMA indicatorconst emaId = await widget.activeChart().createStudy('Moving Average Exponential', false, false, { length: 26 });// Draw an icon and attach it to EMAwidget.chart().createShape(    { time: 1714796562, price: 170 },    {        shape: "icon",        icon: 0xf0da,        ownerStudyId: emaId,    })

The attached drawing follows the indicator when the indicator is moved to another [pane](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/getting_started/glossary#pane)
. If you delete the indicator, all attached drawings are also deleted.

By default, drawings are displayed on the pane with the main [series](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/getting_started/glossary#series)
. You can use `ownerStudyId` to display a drawing on a separate pane that contains indicator. For example, the code sample below creates the [_Moving Average Convergence/Divergence (MACD)_](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000502344-macd-moving-average-convergence-divergence/)
 indicator and displays an arrow on the indicator's pane.

    // Create the MACD indicatorconst macdId = await widget.activeChart().createStudy('MACD', false, false, { in_0: 14, in_1: 30, in_3: 'close', in_2: 9 });// Draw an arrow on the MACD panewidget.activeChart().createMultipointShape(    [      {          time: 1701698287,          price: 2,      },      {          time: 1701698287,          price: -1,      },    ],    {        shape: 'arrow',        text: "text",        ownerStudyId: macdId    })

![Drawing on a separate pane](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/assets/images/drawing-on-macd-pane-b0cc552c7eeea9b856466793fc9dc472.png)

Manage drawings[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#manage-drawings "Direct link to Manage drawings")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you create a drawing using either [`createShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createshape)
, [`createMultipointShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createmultipointshape)
, or [`createAnchoredShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createanchoredshape)
, the methods return the drawing's ID. You can use this ID to refer to the drawing and manage it. For example, you can specify if users can select the drawing in the UI or change drawing properties. To do this, use the [`getShapeById`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#getshapebyid)
 method and pass an ID as a parameter. `getShapeById` returns an instance of the [`ILineDataSourceApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ILineDataSourceApi)
 interface that provides an extensive API for controlling the drawing.

The code sample below gets all drawing properties.

    widget.activeChart().getShapeById(id).getProperties();

Get drawings[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#get-drawings "Direct link to Get drawings")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To get a list of all drawings on the chart and their IDs, use the [`getAllShapes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#getallshapes)
 method.

    widget.activeChart().getAllShapes();

Remove drawings[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#remove-drawings "Direct link to Remove drawings")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### removeEntity[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#removeentity "Direct link to removeEntity")

When you create a drawing using either [`createShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createshape)
, [`createMultipointShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createmultipointshape)
, or [`createAnchoredShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createanchoredshape)
, the methods return the drawing's ID. You can use this ID to remove the drawing from the chart. To do this, use the [`removeEntity`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#removeentity)
 method.

    widget.activeChart().removeEntity(id);

### removeAllShapes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#removeallshapes "Direct link to removeAllShapes")

To remove all drawings from the chart, use the [`removeAllShapes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#removeallshapes)
 method.

    widget.activeChart().removeAllShapes();

Drawing events[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#drawing-events "Direct link to Drawing events")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To handle an event raised when a drawing is added to the chart, call the [`subscribe`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/core_concepts/widget-methods#subscribe--unsubscribe)
 method with the [`drawing`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap#drawing)
 parameter.

    widget.subscribe('drawing', (event) => {    console.log(event.value);});

You can also handle action events, such as moving a drawing or changing its properties. To do this, subscribe to [`drawing_event`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap#drawing_event)
.

    widget.subscribe('drawing_event', (id, type) => {    console.log(id, type);});

Drawing Groups API[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#drawing-groups-api "Direct link to Drawing Groups API")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can combine drawings into groups and manage them as single objects using the [group ID](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Charting_Library#shapesgroupid)
. To do this, call the [`shapesGroupController`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#shapesgroupcontroller)
 method that returns an instance of the [`IShapesGroupControllerApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IShapesGroupControllerApi)
 interface. This interface provides methods for controlling the drawing groups. For example, the code sample below creates a new group from the selected drawings.

    widget.activeChart().shapesGroupController().createGroupFromSelection();

When working with groups, consider the following rules:

1.  Each drawing tool may or may not be part of a specific group. Drawings cannot be part of several groups at the same time.
2.  Only drawings of the same pane can be grouped.
3.  A group cannot be empty. Empty groups are automatically removed.
4.  All drawings in the group have sequential z-indexes. Thus, no other object can be placed between drawings in a group.
5.  Groups are bound with symbols.

*   [Create drawings](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#create-drawings)
    *   [createShape](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createshape)
        
    *   [createMultipointShape](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createmultipointshape)
        
    *   [createExecutionShape](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createexecutionshape)
        
    *   [createAnchoredShape](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#createanchoredshape)
        
*   [Attach drawing to indicator](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#attach-drawing-to-indicator)
    
*   [Manage drawings](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#manage-drawings)
    
*   [Get drawings](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#get-drawings)
    
*   [Remove drawings](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#remove-drawings)
    *   [removeEntity](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#removeentity)
        
    *   [removeAllShapes](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#removeallshapes)
        
*   [Drawing events](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#drawing-events)
    
*   [Drawing Groups API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/ui_elements/drawings/drawings-api/#drawing-groups-api)

---

### Nominal[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker/#nominal "Direct link to Nominal")

Ƭ **Nominal**<`T`, `Name`\>: `T` & { `[species]`: `Name` }

This is the generic type useful for declaring a nominal type, which does not structurally matches with the base type and the other types declared over the same base type

Usage:

**`Example`**

    type Index = Nominal<number, 'Index'>;// let i: Index = 42; // this fails to compilelet i: Index = 42 as Index; // OK

**`Example`**

    type TagName = Nominal<string, 'TagName'>;

#### Type parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker/#type-parameters-1 "Direct link to Type parameters")

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `T` | `T` |
| `Name` | extends `string` |

### TradableSolutions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker/#tradablesolutions "Direct link to TradableSolutions")

Ƭ **TradableSolutions**: [`ChangeAccountSolution`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.ChangeAccountSolution)
 | [`ChangeSymbolSolution`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.ChangeSymbolSolution)
 | [`OpenUrlSolution`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.OpenUrlSolution)

`TradableSolutions` has one of the following keys:

*   `changeAccount` - id of a sub-account suitable for trading the symbol
*   `changeSymbol` - the symbol suitable for trading with current sub-account
*   `openUrl` - the object with URL to be opened and text for solution button

* * *

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.IChartingLibraryWidget

The main interface for interacting with the library, returned by [ChartingLibraryWidgetConstructor](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetConstructor)
. For more information, refer to the [Widget methods](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/core_concepts/widget-methods)
 article.

Methods[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#methods "Direct link to Methods")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### activeChart[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#activechart "Direct link to activeChart")

▸ **activeChart**(): [`IChartWidgetApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi)

Get an API object for interacting with the active chart.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns "Direct link to Returns")

[`IChartWidgetApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi)

An API object for interacting with the chart.

* * *

### activeChartIndex[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#activechartindex "Direct link to activeChartIndex")

▸ **activeChartIndex**(): `number`

Get the index of the active chart in the layout.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

number.

* * *

### addCustomCSSFile[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#addcustomcssfile "Direct link to addCustomCSSFile")

▸ **addCustomCSSFile**(`url`): `void`

Add a custom CSS file for the library to load.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `url` | `string` | A url to the custom CSS file. Should be absolute or relative to the `static` folder. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### applyOverrides[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#applyoverrides "Direct link to applyOverrides")

▸ **applyOverrides**<`TOverrides`\>(`overrides`): `void`

Apply overrides to the chart without reloading. See also [ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions.overrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#overrides)
.

#### Type parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `TOverrides` | extends `Partial`<[`ChartPropertiesOverrides`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartPropertiesOverrides)<br>\> |

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `overrides` | `TOverrides` | An object of overrides to apply to the chart. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### applyStudiesOverrides[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#applystudiesoverrides "Direct link to applyStudiesOverrides")

▸ **applyStudiesOverrides**(`overrides`): `void`

Apply overrides to indicator styles and inputs without reloading. Refer to [Indicator Overrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/customization/overrides/Studies-Overrides#change-default-properties-on-the-fly)
 for more information. Overrides for built-in indicators are listed in [StudyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyOverrides)
.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `overrides` | `object` | An object of overrides to apply to the studies. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### changeTheme[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#changetheme "Direct link to changeTheme")

▸ **changeTheme**(`themeName`, `options?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

Change the theme of the chart.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-3 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `themeName` | [`ThemeName`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#themename) | A theme name. |
| `options?` | [`ChangeThemeOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChangeThemeOptions) | An optional object of options for the theme. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

A promise that resolves when the theme has been changed.

▸ **chartsCount**(): `number`

### clearUndoHistory[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#clearundohistory "Direct link to clearUndoHistory")

▸ **clearUndoHistory**(): `void`

Clears the undo & redo history.

**Warning:** this should only be used in very specific cases where you have considered the UX implications. It is generally unexpected for the user that the undo history has been cleared.

An example of an acceptable use-case would be reusing a chart when switching pages / tabs on a Single Page Application, and presenting it to the user as a new chart.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-8 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### closePopupsAndDialogs[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#closepopupsanddialogs "Direct link to closePopupsAndDialogs")

▸ **closePopupsAndDialogs**(): `void`

Close all open context menus, pop-ups or dialogs.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-9 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### createButton[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#createbutton "Direct link to createButton")

▸ **createButton**(`options?`): `HTMLElement`

Create a button in the top toolbar. This should be called after [headerReady](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#headerready)
 has resolved.

**Example**

    widget.headerReady().then(function() {    var button = widget.createButton();    button.setAttribute('title', 'My custom button tooltip');    button.addEventListener('click', function() { alert("My custom button pressed!"); });    button.textContent = 'My custom button caption';});

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-5 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `options?` | [`CreateHTMLButtonOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateHTMLButtonOptions) | A optional object of options for the button. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-10 "Direct link to Returns")

`HTMLElement`

A `HTMLElement` you can customize.

▸ **createButton**(`options?`): `void`

Create a button in the top toolbar. This should be called after [headerReady](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#headerready)
 has resolved. If the `title` option is provided then the title text will be shown in a tooltip on hover. If the `onClick` option is provided then the button will be clickable.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-6 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `options?` | [`CreateTradingViewStyledButtonOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CreateTradingViewStyledButtonOptions) | A optional object of options for the button. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-11 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

▸ **createButton**(`options?`): `HTMLElement`

Create a button in the top toolbar. This should be called after [headerReady](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#headerready)
 has resolved.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-7 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `options?` | [`CreateButtonOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#createbuttonoptions) | A optional object of options for the button. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-12 "Direct link to Returns")

`HTMLElement`

A `HTMLElement` if the `useTradingViewStyle` option if `false`. `undefined` if `useTradingViewStyle` is `true`.

* * *

### createDropdown[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#createdropdown "Direct link to createDropdown")

▸ **createDropdown**(`params`): `Promise`<[`IDropdownApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDropdownApi)
\>

Add a custom dropdown menu to the top toolbar.

**Example**

    widget.createDropdown(    {        title: 'dropdown',        tooltip: 'tooltip for this dropdown',        items: [            {                title: 'item#1',                onSelect: () => {console.log('1');},            },            {                title: 'item#2',                onSelect: () => {widget.setSymbol('IBM', '1D');},            },            {                title: 'item#3',                onSelect: () => {                    widget.activeChart().createStudy(                        'MACD',                        false,                        false,                        {                            in_0: 14,                            in_1: 30,                            in_3: 'close',                            in_2: 9                        }                    );                },            }        ],        icon: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="28" height="28"><g fill="none" stroke="currentColor"><circle cx="10" cy="10" r="2.5"/><circle cx="18" cy="18" r="2.5"/><path stroke-linecap="square" d="M17.5 7.5l-7 13"/></g></svg>`,    }).then(myDropdownApi => {    // Use myDropdownApi if you need to update the dropdown:    // myDropdownApi.applyOptions({    //     title: 'a new title!'    // });    // Or remove the dropdown:    // myDropdownApi.remove();});

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-8 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `params` | [`DropdownParams`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DropdownParams) |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-13 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`IDropdownApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDropdownApi)
\>

* * *

### crosshairSync[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#crosshairsync "Direct link to crosshairSync")

▸ **crosshairSync**(): [`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValue)
<`boolean`\>

Only available in Trading Platform. Get a watched value that can be used to read/write/subscribe to the state of the crosshair sync between charts.

**Example**

    widget.crosshairSync().setValue(true);

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-14 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValue)
<`boolean`\>

A watched value of the state of the crosshair sync.

* * *

### currencyAndUnitVisibility[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#currencyandunitvisibility "Direct link to currencyAndUnitVisibility")

▸ **currencyAndUnitVisibility**(): [`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValue)
<[`VisibilityType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/enums/Charting_Library.VisibilityType)
\>

Get a watched value that can be used to read/write/subscribe to the state of the currency and unit visibility setting on the price scale.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-15 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValue)
<[`VisibilityType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/enums/Charting_Library.VisibilityType)
\>

A watched value of the state of the currency and unit visibility option.

* * *

### customSymbolStatus[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#customsymbolstatus "Direct link to customSymbolStatus")

▸ **customSymbolStatus**(): [`ICustomSymbolStatusApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ICustomSymbolStatusApi)

Get an API object for creating, and adjusting, custom status items to be displayed within the legend for the main series of each chart.

This can only be accessed when the chart has been created. ([headerReady](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#headerready)
)

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-16 "Direct link to Returns")

[`ICustomSymbolStatusApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ICustomSymbolStatusApi)

An API object for controlling additional custom status items within the legend area.

* * *

### dateFormat[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#dateformat "Direct link to dateFormat")

▸ **dateFormat**(): [`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValue)
<`"dd MMM 'yy"` | `"MMM dd, yyyy"` | `"MMM yyyy"` | `"MMM dd"` | `"dd MMM"` | `"yyyy-MM-dd"` | `"yy-MM-dd"` | `"yy/MM/dd"` | `"yyyy/MM/dd"` | `"dd-MM-yyyy"` | `"dd-MM-yy"` | `"dd/MM/yy"` | `"dd/MM/yyyy"` | `"MM/dd/yy"` | `"MM/dd/yyyy"`\>

Get a watched value that can be used to read/write/subscribe to the state of the date format.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-17 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValue)
<`"dd MMM 'yy"` | `"MMM dd, yyyy"` | `"MMM yyyy"` | `"MMM dd"` | `"dd MMM"` | `"yyyy-MM-dd"` | `"yy-MM-dd"` | `"yy/MM/dd"` | `"yyyy/MM/dd"` | `"dd-MM-yyyy"` | `"dd-MM-yy"` | `"dd/MM/yy"` | `"dd/MM/yyyy"` | `"MM/dd/yy"` | `"MM/dd/yyyy"`\>

A watched value of the state of the date format.

* * *

### dateRangeSync[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#daterangesync "Direct link to dateRangeSync")

<`boolean`\>

▸ **getCSSCustomPropertyValue**(`customPropertyName`): `string`

Returns the current value for a CSS custom property.

**Example:**

    const currentValue = widget.getCSSCustomPropertyValue('--my-theme-color');

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-9 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `customPropertyName` | `string` | A string representing the CSS custom property name to be checked. It is expected that the name should start with a double hyphen ('--'). |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-20 "Direct link to Returns")

`string`

A string containing the value of the property. If not set, returns the empty string.

.

`string`\[\]

**Example**

    console.log(widget.getTheme());

### onChartReady[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#onchartready "Direct link to onChartReady")

▸ **onChartReady**(`callback`): `void`

The library will call `callback` when the chart is ready to be used.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-15 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `callback` | [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback) | A function that will be called when the chart is ready to be used. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-41 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### onContextMenu[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#oncontextmenu "Direct link to onContextMenu")

▸ **onContextMenu**(`callback`): `void`

The widget will call the callback function each time the widget wants to display a context menu. See also [ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions.context\_menu](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#context_menu)
.

**Example**

    widget.onChartReady(function() {    widget.onContextMenu(function(unixtime, price) {        return [{            position: "top",            text: "First top menu item, time: " + unixtime + ", price: " + price,            click: function() { alert("First clicked."); }        },        { text: "-", position: "top" }, // Adds a separator between buttons        { text: "-Paste" },             // Removes the existing item from the menu        {            position: "top",            text: "Second top menu item 2",            click: function() { alert("Second clicked."); }        }, {            position: "bottom",            text: "Bottom menu item",            click: function() { alert("Third clicked."); }        }];    });});

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-16 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `callback` | (`unixTime`: `number`, `price`: `number`) => [`ContextMenuItem`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ContextMenuItem)<br>\[\] | A function called with the time and price of the location on the chart that triggered the context menu. The array of objects returned will add or remove items from the context menu. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-42 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### onGrayedObjectClicked[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#ongrayedobjectclicked "Direct link to onGrayedObjectClicked")

▸ **onGrayedObjectClicked**(`callback`): `void`

The library will call `callback` when a greyed-out drawing tool or study is clicked.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-17 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `callback` | (`obj`: [`GrayedObject`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.GrayedObject)<br>) => `void` | A function that will be called when a greyed-out drawing tool or study is clicked. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-43 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### onShortcut[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#onshortcut "Direct link to onShortcut")

▸ **onShortcut**(`shortCut`, `callback`): `void`

The library will call `callback` when the `shortCut` keys are input.

Use a string separated by '+' for shortcuts using an alphabet character (A to Z) with optional modifiers (ctrl, shift, alt). Use a number for shortcuts using non-alphabet character without modifiers. If you don't know the key code you need you can use resources like [keycode.info](https://keycode.info/)
, or [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent/keyCode)
 to check. Use an array of literal key codes and modifier strings for shortcuts using non-alphabet characters with optional modifier strings.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-18 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `shortCut` | `string` \| `number` \| (`string` \| `number`)\[\] | A number, a string, or an array of number and string. |
| `callback` | [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback) | A function that will be called when the `shortCut` keys are input. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-44 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

**`Example`**

    widget.onShortcut("alt+q", function() {  widget.chart().executeActionById("symbolSearch");});// F1widget.onShortcut(112, function() {  widget.chart().executeActionById("symbolSearch");});// ctrl+shift+\widget.onShortcut(['ctrl', 'shift', 220], function() {	 widget.chart().executeActionById("symbolSearch");});

* * *

### paneButtonsVisibility[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#panebuttonsvisibility "Direct link to paneButtonsVisibility")

<[`VisibilityType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/enums/Charting_Library.VisibilityType)
\>

▸ **saveChartToServer**(`onComplete?`, `onFail?`, `options?`): `void`

| `onFail?` | [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback) | An optional callback function called when the chart fails to save. |
| `options?` | [`SaveChartToServerOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SaveChartToServerOptions) | An optional object of options for saving the chart. |

`void`

`void`

Get the currently selected drawing or cursor.

* * *

### setActiveChart[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#setactivechart "Direct link to setActiveChart")

▸ **setActiveChart**(`index`): `void`

Set which chart is currently active. It is recommended that this method is only used when linked to a user action which should change the active chart.

Use [chartsCount](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#chartscount)
 to determine the number of charts currently available. If an invalid index is supplied (less than zero, or greater than the number of charts minus 1) then this method will not change the active chart.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-27 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `index` | `number` | index of chart to set as the active chart. Index is zero-based. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-56 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### setCSSCustomProperty[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#setcsscustomproperty "Direct link to setCSSCustomProperty")

▸ **setCSSCustomProperty**(`customPropertyName`, `value`): `void`

Sets the value for a CSS custom property.

**Example:**

    widget.setCSSCustomProperty('--my-theme-color', '#123AAA');

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-28 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `customPropertyName` | `string` | A string representing the CSS custom property name. It is expected that the name should start with a double hyphen ('--'). |
| `value` | `string` | A string containing the new property value. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-57 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### setDebugMode[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#setdebugmode "Direct link to setDebugMode")

| `enabled` | `boolean` | A boolean flag. `true` to enable debug mode, `false` to disable. |

`void`

| --- | --- | --- |

| `callback` | [`SubscribeEventsMap`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap)<br>\[`EventName`\] | A callback that will be called when the event happens. |

### supportedChartTypes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#supportedcharttypes "Direct link to supportedChartTypes")

▸ **supportedChartTypes**(): [`IWatchedValueReadonly`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValueReadonly)
<[`ChartStyle`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle)
\[\]>

This method returns a readonly WatchedValue ([IWatchedValueReadonly](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValueReadonly)
) object that can be used to read/watch the current supported chart types ([SeriesType](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/enums/Charting_Library.SeriesType)
) for an active chart.

The chart type is returned as a number. You can see which number corresponds to which chart type in the [Overrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/customization/overrides/)
 documentation for `mainSeriesProperties.style`.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-67 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IWatchedValueReadonly`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValueReadonly)
<[`ChartStyle`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle)
\[\]>

* * *

### symbolSync[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#symbolsync "Direct link to symbolSync")

▸ **symbolSync**(): [`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValue)
<`boolean`\>

Only available in Trading Platform. Get a watched value that can be used to read/write/subscribe to the state of the symbol sync between charts.

**Example**

    if (widget.symbolSync().value()) {    // ...}

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-69 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchedValue)
<`boolean`\>

A watched value of the state of the symbol sync.

* * *

### takeClientScreenshot[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#takeclientscreenshot "Direct link to takeClientScreenshot")

▸ **takeClientScreenshot**(`options?`): `Promise`<`HTMLCanvasElement`\>

Create a snapshot of the chart and return it as a canvas.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#parameters-35 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `options?` | `Partial`<[`ClientSnapshotOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ClientSnapshotOptions)<br>\> | An optional object that customizes the returned snapshot. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-70 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`HTMLCanvasElement`\>

A promise containing a `HTMLCanvasElement` of the snapshot.

* * *

### takeScreenshot[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#takescreenshot "Direct link to takeScreenshot")

▸ **takeScreenshot**(): `void`

Create a snapshot of the chart and upload it to the server. When it is ready callback functions subscribed to the `'onScreenshotReady'` event using [subscribe](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#subscribe)
 will be called. The URL of the snapshot will be passed as an argument to the callback function.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-71 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

\>

### watchList[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#watchlist "Direct link to watchList")

▸ **watchList**(): `Promise`<[`IWatchListApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchListApi)
\>

Trading Platform only. Get a promise that resolves with an API object for interacting with the widgetbar (right sidebar) watchlist.

**Example**

    const watchlistApi = await widget.watchList();const activeListId = watchlistApi.getActiveListId();const currentListItems = watchlistApi.getList(activeListId);// append new section and item to the current watchlistwatchlistApi.updateList(activeListId, [...currentListItems, '###NEW SECTION', 'AMZN']);

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-76 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`IWatchListApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatchListApi)
\>

An API object for interacting with the widgetbar (right sidebar) watchlist.

* * *

### watermark[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#watermark "Direct link to watermark")

▸ **watermark**(): [`IWatermarkApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatermarkApi)

Get an API object for adjusting the watermarks present on the charts. This can only be accessed when the chart is ready to be used. ([onChartReady](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#onchartready)
)

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget/#returns-77 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IWatermarkApi`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IWatermarkApi)

An API object for adjusting the watermark settings.

* * *

# Interface: AccountManagerInfo | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v27**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Broker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker)
.AccountManagerInfo

The information object that is used to build the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### accountTitle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#accounttitle "Direct link to accountTitle")

• **accountTitle**: `string`

Name of the broker

* * *

### customFormatters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#customformatters "Direct link to customFormatters")

• `Optional` **customFormatters**: [`CustomTableElementFormatter`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.CustomTableElementFormatter)
<[`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#tableformatterinputvalues)
\>\[\]

An optional array for defining [custom formatters](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/trading_terminal/account-manager/value-formatters#custom-formatters)
. Each formatter description is an object with the following fields:

*   `name` ([FormatterName](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#formattername)
    ): Unique formatter name.
    
*   `formatText` ([TableFormatTextFunction](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#tableformattextfunction)
    ): Function that is used for formatting a cell value to a string. The `formatText` field is required because it is used to generate exported data. You can return an empty string if you do not need this function.
    
*   `formatElement` ([CustomTableFormatElementFunction](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#customtableformatelementfunction)
     | `undefined`): Optional function that is used for formatting a cell value to a string or an HTML element.
    

If the `formatElement` function is provided, it only handles the formatting of displayed values. Otherwise the `formatText` function is used. For optimal performance, it is recommended to only use `formatText` if you intend to display only string values.

**Example**

    {    name: 'closeButton' as FormatterName, // Typecast to FormatterName. Use constant in real code    formatText: () => '', // Returns an empty string because we don't need to display this in the exported data    formatElement: ({ values: [id] }: TableFormatterInputs<[id: string]>) => {        const button = document.createElement('button');        button.innerText = 'Close';        button.addEventListener('click', () => {            event.stopPropagation();            closePosition(id);        });        return button;    },}

* * *

### historyColumns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#historycolumns "Direct link to historyColumns")

• `Optional` **historyColumns**: [`AccountManagerColumn`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#accountmanagercolumn)
\[\]

An array of data objects that create columns for the [History](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/trading_terminal/account-manager/#history)
 page where all orders from previous sessions are shown. Note that this page is only shown if you set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportOrdersHistory](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportordershistory)
 to `true` and implement the [IBrokerTerminal.ordersHistory](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#ordershistory)
 method.

* * *

### historyColumnsSorting[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#historycolumnssorting "Direct link to historyColumnsSorting")

• `Optional` **historyColumnsSorting**: [`SortingParameters`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.SortingParameters)

Optional sorting of the table on the [History](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/trading_terminal/account-manager/#history)
 page.

* * *

### individualPositionColumns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#individualpositioncolumns "Direct link to individualPositionColumns")

• `Optional` **individualPositionColumns**: [`AccountManagerColumn`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#accountmanagercolumn)
\[\]

You can display any field of an [IndividualPosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.IndividualPosition)
 or add your own fields to an individualPosition object and display them.

* * *

### marginUsed[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#marginused "Direct link to marginUsed")

• `Optional` **marginUsed**: [`IWatchedValue`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.IWatchedValue)
<`number`\>

Margin used

* * *

### orderColumns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#ordercolumns "Direct link to orderColumns")

• **orderColumns**: [`OrderTableColumn`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#ordertablecolumn)
\[\]

An array of data objects that create columns for the [Orders](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/trading_terminal/account-manager/#orders-and-positions)
 page. You can display any field of an [Order](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#order)
 or add your own fields to an order object and display them.

* * *

### orderColumnsSorting[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#ordercolumnssorting "Direct link to orderColumnsSorting")

• `Optional` **orderColumnsSorting**: [`SortingParameters`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.SortingParameters)

Optional sorting of the orders table.

* * *

### pages[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#pages "Direct link to pages")

• **pages**: [`AccountManagerPage`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerPage)
\[\]

Adds [custom pages](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/trading_terminal/account-manager/#custom-pages)
 to the Account Manager. Each page is a set of tables.

* * *

### positionColumns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#positioncolumns "Direct link to positionColumns")

• `Optional` **positionColumns**: [`AccountManagerColumn`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#accountmanagercolumn)
\[\]

An array of data objects that create columns for the [Positions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/trading_terminal/account-manager/#orders-and-positions)
 page. You can display any field of a [Position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.Position)
 or add your own fields to a position object and display them.

* * *

### possibleOrderStatuses[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#possibleorderstatuses "Direct link to possibleOrderStatuses")

• `Optional` **possibleOrderStatuses**: [`OrderStatus`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/enums/Broker.OrderStatus)
\[\]

Optional list of statuses to be used in the orders filter. Default list is used if it hasn't been set.

* * *

### summary[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#summary "Direct link to summary")

• **summary**: [`AccountManagerSummaryField`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerSummaryField)
\[\]

Custom fields that are always displayed at the top-right corner of the Account Manager. Refer to the [Account Summary row](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/trading_terminal/account-manager/#account-summary-row)
 section for more information.

Methods[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#methods "Direct link to Methods")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### contextMenuActions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#contextmenuactions "Direct link to contextMenuActions")

▸ **contextMenuActions**(`contextMenuEvent`, `activePageActions`): `Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo)
\[\]>

Optional function to create a custom context menu.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `contextMenuEvent` | `MouseEvent` \| `TouchEvent` | MouseEvent or TouchEvent object passed by a browser |
| `activePageActions` | [`ActionMetaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo)<br>\[\] | array of `ActionMetaInfo` items for the current page |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo)
\[\]>

`Promise` that is resolved with an array of `ActionMetaInfo`

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#properties)
    *   [accountTitle](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#accounttitle)
        
    *   [customFormatters](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#customformatters)
        
    *   [historyColumns](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#historycolumns)

    *   [marginUsed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#marginused)
        
    *   [orderColumns](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#ordercolumns)
        
    *   [orderColumnsSorting](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#ordercolumnssorting)
        
    *   [pages](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#pages)
        
    *   [positionColumns](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#positioncolumns)
        
    *   [possibleOrderStatuses](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo/#possibleorderstatuses)

# Interface: Position | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v27**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.Position

Describes a single position.

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`PositionBase`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase)
    
*   [`CustomFields`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CustomFields)
    
    ↳ **`Position`**
    

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### avgPrice[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#avgprice "Direct link to avgPrice")

• **avgPrice**: `number`

Average price

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[PositionBase](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase)
.[avgPrice](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase#avgprice)

* * *

### id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#id "Direct link to id")

• **id**: `string`

Position ID. Usually id should be equal to brokerSymbol

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[PositionBase](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase)
.[id](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase#id)

* * *

### longQty[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#longqty "Direct link to longQty")

• `Optional` **longQty**: `number`

Long position quantity

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[PositionBase](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase)
.[longQty](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase#longqty)

* * *

### message[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#message "Direct link to message")

• `Optional` **message**: [`OrderOrPositionMessage`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OrderOrPositionMessage)

Message describing the state of the position

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#inherited-from-3 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[PositionBase](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase)
.[message](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase#message)

* * *

### qty[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#qty "Direct link to qty")

• **qty**: `number`

Position Quantity (positive number)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position/#inherited-from-4 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[PositionBase](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase)
.[qty](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionBase#qty)

# Toolbars | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/ui_elements/Toolbars/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

### Buttons[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/ui_elements/Toolbars/#buttons "Direct link to Buttons")

You can add your own buttons to the top toolbar.

[`createButton(options)`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#createbutton)

[`createDropdown(options)`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#createdropdown)

#### Accessible Buttons[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/ui_elements/Toolbars/#accessible-buttons "Direct link to Accessible Buttons")

Custom buttons created with TradingView's built-in style using [the `createButton` overload](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#createbutton)
 will benefit from the library's [accessible keyboard navigation added in version 26.002](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/releases/release-notes#version-26002)
.

Custom buttons created using the [non-TradingView style `createButton` overload](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#createbutton)
 will not benefit from keyboard accessibility features.

### Styles[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/ui_elements/Toolbars/#styles "Direct link to Styles")

The library has 2 predefined color themes. They are optimal for displaying the charts. However, since version 16 you can change the colors of the toolbars to perfectly fit to your background. You can see an example of the customization in [themes.html](https://github.com/tradingview/charting_library/blob/master/themed.html "The repository is private. Click the lock icon to see the 'Getting Access' instruction.")
 [🔐 (restricted access)](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/getting_started/quick-start#getting-access "Click to open the 'Getting Access' section.")
.

*   [Buttons](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/ui_elements/Toolbars/#buttons)
    
*   [Styles](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/ui_elements/Toolbars/#styles)

---

.ClearMarksMode

Mode to clear the marks on the chart.

Enumeration Members[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/enums/Charting_Library.ClearMarksMode/#enumeration-members "Direct link to Enumeration Members")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### All[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/enums/Charting_Library.ClearMarksMode/#all "Direct link to All")

• **All** = `0`

Will clear both bar marks AND timescale marks - default value

* * *

### BarMarks[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/enums/Charting_Library.ClearMarksMode/#barmarks "Direct link to BarMarks")

• **BarMarks** = `1`

Will only clear bar marks

* * *

### TimeScaleMarks[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/enums/Charting_Library.ClearMarksMode/#timescalemarks "Direct link to TimeScaleMarks")

• **TimeScaleMarks** = `2`

Will only clear timescale marks
